name: APT28
attribution: GRU 85th Main Special Service Center (GTsSS), military unit 26165
description: APT28 (also tracked as Fancy Bear, Forest Blizzard, STRONTIUM, Pawn Storm, Sednit, and Sofacy) is a Russian military intelligence (GRU) cyber espionage group attributed to Unit 26165 of the 85th Main Special Service Center (GTsSS). Active since at least 2004, APT28 conducts intelligence collection aligned with Russian military and geopolitical objectives. The group is known for sophisticated spearphishing campaigns, zero-day exploitation, and credential harvesting operations targeting government, military, defense, political organizations, media, and anti-doping agencies across NATO member states and former Soviet countries. APT28 gained widespread public attention for its role in the 2016 US presidential election interference, including the DNC breach and subsequent hack-and-leak operations. In recent years, the group has expanded targeting to Western logistics and technology companies supporting Ukraine, as documented in the May 2025 joint advisory (CISA AA25-141A). The group demonstrates sustained evolution in tradecraft, including the development of custom exploit tools (GooseEgg), novel access techniques (Nearest Neighbor Wi-Fi attack), and webmail exploitation campaigns (Operation RoundPress).

persistence.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys

detection.title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
detection.content: Monitor for powershell.exe launched with -enc, -encodedcommand, or -e flags containing Base64 payloads. Enable Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104) and Module Logging. Sigma rule: proc_creation_win_susp_powershell_enc_cmd. KQL: DeviceProcessEvents | where FileName == 'powershell.exe' and ProcessCommandLine contains '-enc'

detection.title: Outlook NTLM Relay (CVE-2023-23397)
detection.content: Detect outbound SMB connections initiated by Outlook calendar processing. Monitor for Windows Event ID 4648 (logon with explicit credentials) to external IPs. Alert on UNC path access from outlook.exe. KQL: DeviceNetworkEvents | where InitiatingProcessFileName == 'outlook.exe' and RemotePort == 445

detection.title: LSASS Credential Dumping
detection.content: Monitor for processes accessing lsass.exe memory. Sysmon Event ID 10 with TargetImage containing lsass.exe and GrantedAccess of 0x1010 or 0x1038. Deploy Credential Guard where possible. Sigma rule: sysmon_cred_dump_lsass_access.

detection.title: GooseEgg Print Spooler Exploitation (CVE-2022-38028)
detection.content: Monitor svchost.exe (spoolsv) spawning unexpected child processes like cmd.exe or powershell.exe. Track file modifications in C:\Windows\System32\spool\drivers\. Microsoft published specific detection guidance for GooseEgg exploitation.

detection.title: Encoded PowerShell via WMI
detection.content: Monitor for WMI spawning powershell.exe with encoded arguments. Sysmon Event ID 1 with ParentImage wmiprvse.exe. Elastic detection rule: command_and_scripting_interpreter_via_wmi.

detection.title: Suspicious Outlook Behavior
detection.content: Monitor for Outlook spawning script interpreters or making outbound SMB connections. ASR rule: Block Office applications from creating executable content. Elastic rule: microsoft_365_exchange_transport_rule_creation.

detection.title: Registry Autorun Modification
detection.content: Monitor HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run for new entries. Sysmon Event ID 13 (Registry value set). Sigma rule: registry_set_asep_reg_keys_modification_currentversion.

related_actors.name: Sandworm
related_actors.desc: Both GRU but different units (26165 vs 74455). Occasional shared infrastructure.

related_actors.name: Turla
related_actors.desc: FSB group. Documented cases of Turla piggybacking on APT28 infrastructure.

defense_recommendations.text: Deploy phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn) for all accounts, prioritizing email and VPN. APT28 credential harvesting bypasses SMS/TOTP MFA.
Patch Outlook CVE-2023-23397 and Print Spooler CVE-2022-38028 immediately. Both actively exploited by APT28 in ongoing campaigns.
Enable PowerShell Constrained Language Mode and Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104). Block encoded command execution via ASR rules.
Block outbound NTLM to external IPs at network perimeter. APT28 uses NTLM relay via Outlook calendar invites.
Deploy Credential Guard on Windows 10/11 to protect LSASS from memory dumping. Monitor LSASS access via Sysmon Event ID 10.
Segment email infrastructure from Active Directory domain controllers. APT28 pivots from mailboxes to DC via pass-the-hash.
Enforce DMARC p=reject on all organizational domains to prevent email spoofing in spearphishing campaigns.

threat_assessment.summary: Tier 1 nation-state actor with demonstrated zero-day capability, global reach, and destructive intent when aligned with Russian military objectives.

kill_chain.mapping: Open-source research on targets, credential harvesting via phishing portals, LinkedIn profiling
Weaponized Office documents with VBA macros, EPS exploits (CVE-2017-0262), GooseEgg exploit tool
Spearphishing emails with attachments/links, watering hole sites, Wi-Fi proximity attacks (Nearest Neighbor)
Zebrocy, XAgent, Komplex (macOS), registry run keys, scheduled tasks
HTTPS C2 via compromised WordPress sites, X-Tunnel for encrypted relay, Tor for anonymization
Email exfiltration, credential harvesting, hack-and-leak operations, intelligence collection on military/political targets
